questions.question: A body of mass 5 kg is initially at rest. A net force of 20 N acts on it for 4 s. What will be its final velocity?
Which of the following best explains why passengers fall forward when a moving bus stops suddenly?
The SI unit of momentum is
A force of 30 N produces an acceleration of 6 m/s² in an object. What is the mass of the object?
Which quantity remains conserved during an isolated collision?
An object moving with uniform velocity will continue moving with the same velocity if
options: A balanced force acts on it.
No net external force acts on it.
A large force acts on it.
questions.answer: No net external force acts on it.

questions.question: The rate of change of momentum of a body is directly proportional to
A cricket player pulls his hands backward while catching a fast-moving ball. This action primarily
options: Increases the force on the hands.
Reduces the impact force by increasing the time of contact.
Increases the momentum of the ball.
questions.answer: Reduces the impact force by increasing the time of contact.

questions.question: A truck and a car are moving with the same velocity. Which has greater momentum?

A body of mass 2 kg is moving with a velocity of 8 m/s. Its momentum is
A force of 50 N acts on a body for 2 s. The impulse delivered to the body is
A batsman moves his bat backward while hitting a cricket ball. This helps to
options: Increase the time of contact and hence the impulse.
Reduce the momentum of the bat.
questions.answer: Increase the time of contact and hence the impulse.

questions.question: Which of the following situations best demonstrates Newton's Third Law of Motion?
options: A book resting on a table.
A car moving at constant speed.

questions.question: An object experiences an acceleration of 5 m/s² when a force of 25 N is applied. What force is required to produce an acceleration of 8 m/s² in the same object?
A body of mass 4 kg moving with a velocity of 6 m/s is brought to rest. The change in momentum is
Why are airbags installed in cars?
options: To increase the speed of the passenger.
To increase the force during collision.
To increase the time of impact and reduce injury.
To reduce the mass of the vehicle.
questions.answer: To increase the time of impact and reduce injury.
